title: Implement Spec Discovery And Frontmatter Utilities
description: Add focused helpers for locating spec files across lifecycle folders, resolving IDs and partial filenames case-insensitively, parsing and serializing YAML frontmatter, identifying legacy specs without frontmatter, and inferring display names from headings or filenames.
acceptance_criteria: The CLI can search across `specs/drafts/`, `specs/planned/`, and `specs/done/`.
Resolution by full `id` or partial filename is case-insensitive and reports ambiguous and missing matches with the specified messages.
Malformed YAML frontmatter is detected and surfaced as the specified parse error.
Legacy specs with no frontmatter are recognized distinctly from malformed-frontmatter specs.
Utility functions preserve existing non-target content when frontmatter is rewritten.

handoff_summary.completed: Added to `skills/spec-management/spec.py`: `parse_frontmatter()` (returns None for legacy specs, raises FrontmatterError for malformed YAML), `is_legacy_spec()`, `infer_display_name()` (frontmatter name → first H1 heading → filename stem), `write_frontmatter()` (preserves body when rewriting), `find_all_specs()` (discovers .md files across all three lifecycle dirs), `resolve_spec()` (case-insensitive exact ID then partial filename match, with correct error messages for ambiguous/missing). Full CLI commands implemented: `cmd_create`, `cmd_list`, `cmd_show`, `cmd_set`, `cmd_migrate`.
handoff_summary.remaining: Test file `tests/test_spec_cli.py` — creation of tests is out of scope for this developer bead and is owned by the tester bead.
handoff_summary.risks: The `write_frontmatter` function re-serializes YAML with PyYAML, which may reformat YAML style (e.g. inline lists become block lists). This is intentional and matches the spec behaviour. The `spec set status` command rewrites frontmatter twice (at old location, then again at new) when moving files; logically harmless but slightly redundant — can be cleaned up if needed.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester bead should cover: `parse_frontmatter` returns None for legacy specs, raises FrontmatterError for malformed YAML; `resolve_spec` error messages for ambiguous and missing; `write_frontmatter` body preservation; `is_legacy_spec` true/false; `cmd_list` --status/--tag/--priority filters; `cmd_set status` file move + frontmatter update; `cmd_create` duplicate-file error.

execution_history.summary: Implemented spec discovery, frontmatter parsing/serialization, legacy spec detection, ID/filename resolution, and display name inference utilities in `skills/spec-management/spec.py`. Also implemented all CLI commands (`create`, `list`, `show`, `set`, `migrate`) built on top of these utilities.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
